facts.subject: smoke_test_result
facts.object: b07 (timeout, retry succeeded), b16 (cache miss turn 2) — both pre-existing, non-blocking

facts.subject: v1.5_post_deploy
facts.object: 10-minute watch on error rates + latency, then standard cadence

must_preserve: V1.5 is live in production (k8s rollout complete on the v1.5 image)
Smoke test passed 23/25 with 2 non-blocking flaky failures (b07 timeout, b16 cache miss)
Post-deploy monitoring active for 10 minutes then standard cadence
expected_failure_modes: Tool-call clutter: this conversation has 12+ tool_call/tool_result pairs. Compaction Pass B (tool-result pruning) interacts here — does the right tool result survive?
The 23/25 specific number is a fact; compaction often loses precision on numbers in tool output
The flaky-but-passing nature of b07 is nuanced — easy to lose the 'retry succeeded' caveat and either drop the failure or treat it as a real failure
The two failures (b07, b16) are pre-existing — that context matters. Compaction may forget the 'pre-existing' qualifier and look like NEW failures introduced by V1.5
